name: Review your notification
pageTitle: Review Notification - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 - CHEDD
heading: Review your notification
bodyCopy: You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.
secondaryActions: Change (many, one per summary section — links back to the relevant edit page with ?fromImporterReview=true)
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (with nested govuk-grid-column-one-half for the reference/audit header block)

structure.govukComponent: Caption (rendered in the service sub-header bar, not a govuk-caption-xl in main)

structure.text: Please fix the following errors — govuk-error-summary rendered at the top of the review (unless overdue-debtor) whenever the notification has outstanding validation errors that block Save and continue. Each error is a link to the offending section (href=#{field}). NOT rendered in either happy-path trace.
Last updated 16 July 2026, 17:18 / by Michael Scott
CHED reference DRAFT.GB.2026.1526057 [Copy] / Reference for your customs declaration GBCHD2026.1526057 [Copy] / Customs document code C678 [Copy]
structure.govukComponent: Summary list (govuk-summary-list) with copy-button widgets in the value column

structure.text: You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.

Import type: High risk food of non-animal origin / Country of origin: Afghanistan [Change] / Country from where consigned: Afghanistan / Consignment reference number: (empty) / Main reason for importing the consignment: For internal market [Change] / [non-internal market only] Point of exit: Manchester / [non-internal market only] When the consignment will leave Great Britain: 19 July 2026, 12:00

structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table, data-table-commodities) — one row per commodity, each with its own Change link

Total net weight 1 kg/units / Total packages 1 / Total gross weight 11 kg/units
Commodity intended for: Feedingstuff / Temperature: Ambient
columns: Document type | Document reference | Date of issue | Attachments; row: Health certificate | REF-124 | 4 March 2023 | example.png (download link)
Consignor or exporter: Linus George Ltd, 101 Main St, ... [Change] / Consignee: Global Corp, 102 High St, ... / Importer: Global Corp, ... / Place of Destination: Global Corp, ...
structure.govukComponent: NON-STANDARD (review-summary-list) — each value is a name line + a comma-joined address/contact line

structure.text: Transport to the port of entry

Transport to the BCP or Port of entry — row headers: Port of entry MANCHESTER AIRPORT (GBMAN) / Means of transport to port of entry Railway / Transport identification F12345 / Using road trailers or containers? No / Transport document reference certificate / Estimated arrival date at BCP or Port of entry 18 July 2026 / Estimated arrival time at port of entry 19:18
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table, presentation-table) — key/value rows via rowheader + cell

structure.text: Using the Common Transit Convention (CTC) No / Using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS) No
Name: Michael Scott / Email address: test14@hghumankainos.33mail.com / Phone number: 01234566234
Summary of nominated contacts — columns: Name | Email address | Mobile number (no data rows in this trace)
Contact address 105 Broadway, Suite 3, Building 10, Greenville, 10002, 01100100102

govukComponents.usage: H1 page title (xl); section headings (l) e.g. 'About the consignment', 'Description of the goods', 'Traders', 'Transport', 'Contacts', 'Documents'; sub-section headings (m) e.g. 'Additional details', 'Transport to the port of entry', 'Goods movement services', 'Contact details', 'Nominated contacts', 'Contact address for consignment'

The reference/code header block (CHED reference, Reference for your customs declaration, Customs document code) — the ONE section using the standard component; every other 'summary list' on the page is the custom review-summary-list
Warns that the correct reference/code must be used on the customs declaration or the consignment will be delayed

LEGACY: rendered above the reference block whenever the notification has outstanding validation errors that block submission. Title defaults to 'Please fix the following errors'; cloned-CVEDA and other external-system variants override the title/help text. Each list item links to the offending section. NOT exercised in either happy-path trace.

Multiple read-only summary tables: commodity code list, consignment net-weight/packages, additional-documents, transport-to-port key/value, goods-movement-services, nominated-contacts, contact-address. Each carries an additional bespoke class (review-table, presentation-table, data-table-commodities, review-additional-documents-table)
Primary 'Save and continue' submit button (proceeds to declaration); three secondary 'Copy' buttons in the reference header block
Visible horizontal rule separating the reference/warning header block from 'About the consignment'
Full-width page column; the last-updated audit-flag and status use a one-half split at the top
'Change' links per section (deep-link back into the edit journey with ?fromImporterReview=true) and the attachment download link

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A bespoke re-implementation of the GOV.UK Summary list used for most key/value review sections (About the consignment, Additional details, Traders, Contact details)
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Parallel hand-rolled component duplicating govuk-summary-list; drifts from the Design System (own row/border/action modifiers) and must be maintained separately. The new app should render these as standard govuk Summary lists.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Copy-to-clipboard button beside each reference/code value in the header block
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A JS clipboard widget not in the Design System; requires client-side JS to function and has no no-JS fallback shown. Rebuild can use a govuk-button plus a small clipboard script, or drop it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Button (govuk-button--secondary) wired to the Clipboard API; no first-party govuk copy component exists
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 100 (class inventory); public/javascripts/copy_reference_number.js referenced review.html:356

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the 'Last updated 16 July 2026, 17:18 / by Michael Scott' audit line at the top of the review
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom audit metadata block; purely presentational styling not in the toolbox. Trivially replaceable with govuk body text.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Body text (govuk-body / govuk-body-s) or a small summary list
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 100 (class inventory); {{> common/auditFlag}} review.html:88

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Key/value tables built with row-header + cell pairs (Transport to the BCP, Goods movement services, Contact address)
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke table variant used to render vertical key/value pairs where a Summary list would be idiomatic. In the new app these read more naturally as Summary lists than as tables.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Summary list (or govuk-table if a tabular layout is genuinely wanted)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom styling classes layered on the goods, documents and commodity tables (incl. a responsive-table helper)
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Extra IPAFFS-specific table CSS beyond govuk-table; the responsive helper suggests horizontal-scroll handling the Design System table does not provide out of the box. Keep as a plain govuk Table wrapped in an overflow container.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Table (govuk-table) inside an overflow-x scroll container

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Flex layout that places a 'Change' link inline beside a section heading (e.g. 'Additional details Change', 'Documents ... Change')
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom layout to attach an action to a heading; not a Design System pattern. Standard approach is the summary-list action column or a heading followed by a link.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-summary-list__actions, or a heading plus a govuk-link

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: packages / package-type / weight / ellipsis / break-word / border-bottom-none
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Small utility/styling classes for table cells (column widths, text truncation/wrapping, border suppression)
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Minor bespoke CSS utilities; mostly replaceable by govuk width-override classes and normal cell styling. Low concern individually but part of a wider custom-CSS footprint on this page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-!-width-* overrides and default govuk-table cell styling

fields.name: countryOfOrigin
fields.label: Country of origin
fields.hint: Change link -> /consignment/page-2?fromImporterReview=true
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: Commodities.java:168-180 — commodities.countryOfOrigin @NotNull groups are HighRisk / CHEDPP / CvedaEu / HighRiskEuCed / CvedpEu; NotificationSingleCedValidation is NOT among them
fields.notes: Legacy: NOT tagged @NotNull for the GB single CHED-D (SingleCed) submit group, so the old system did not block submission on a missing country of origin at this level. required left false. Whether the rebuild should make country of origin mandatory for CHED-D is a policy question for a human.

fields.name: countryFromWhereConsigned
fields.label: Country from where consigned
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: Commodities.java:189-201 — commodities.consignedCountry @NotNull groups are CedOrCvedp / CHEDPP / HighRiskEuCed / CvedpEu; NotificationSingleCedValidation is NOT listed
fields.notes: Legacy: NOT tagged @NotNull for the GB single CHED-D (SingleCed) submit group. required left false; a policy the rebuild may revisit.

fields.name: consignmentReferenceNumber
fields.label: Consignment reference number
fields.hint: Empty in both observed traces
fields.notes: No @NotNull validation-group binding found for this field in PartOne — optional in legacy (consistent with the empty rendered value).

fields.name: mainReasonForImporting
fields.label: Main reason for importing the consignment
fields.hint: Change link -> /consignment/page-5?fromImporterReview=true
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 100; trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 104
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:359-362 (purpose @NotNull groups = NotificationSingleCedValidation); ValidationMessages.properties:28 singleced.purpose.not.null = 'Select the main reason for importing the consignment'
fields.notes: Legacy: purpose is mandatory for GB CHED-D submission. required set true to reflect the underlying model @NotNull; this is display-only on the review page.

fields.name: pointOfExit
fields.label: Point of exit
fields.conditionalOn: Main reason for importing = 'Non-internal market' (transit/re-export). Absent when 'For internal market'. Mandatory only in the non-internal-market branch.
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: Purpose.java:97-100 (pointOfExit @NotNull group = NotNullPointOfExitValidation); PartOneFieldValidator.java:167-170 adds NotNullPointOfExitValidation when shouldApplyNonInternalMarketValidation; ValidationMessages.properties:205 purpose.pointOfExit.not.null = 'Select the point of exit'
fields.notes: Legacy: mandatory for CHED-D ONLY when the non-internal-market (transit/re-export) branch applies. required=true is conditional (see conditionalOn).

fields.name: leaveGreatBritainDate
fields.label: When the consignment will leave Great Britain
fields.hint: Rendered value combines the date and time (e.g. '19 July 2026, 12:00'); the legacy model stores them as two separate mandatory fields — estimatedArrivalDateAtPortOfExit and estimatedArrivalTimeAtPortOfExit — see leaveGreatBritainTime.
fields.conditionalOn: Main reason for importing = 'Non-internal market'. Absent when 'For internal market'.
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: Purpose.java:81-86 (estimatedArrivalDateAtPortOfExit @NotNull group = NotNullEstimatedArrivalDateValidation); PartOneFieldValidator.java:167-171 adds it in the non-internal-market branch; ValidationMessages.properties:206 purpose.estimatedArrivalDateAtPortOfExit.not.null = 'Enter the date the consignment will leave Great Britain'
fields.notes: Legacy: mandatory for CHED-D ONLY in the non-internal-market branch.

fields.name: leaveGreatBritainTime
fields.label: When the consignment will leave Great Britain (time component)
fields.hint: LEGACY-ADDED: the model splits the 'leave Great Britain' datetime into a separate time field. The review renders it inline with the date (the '...12:00' portion of '19 July 2026, 12:00'), so it is not a distinct visible row — but it is a distinct mandatory datum in the non-internal-market branch.
fields.evidence: Purpose.java:89-94 (estimatedArrivalTimeAtPortOfExit @NotNull group = NotNullEstimatedArrivalTimeValidation); PartOneFieldValidator.java:167-171; ValidationMessages.properties:207 purpose.estimatedArrivalTimeAtPortOfExit.not.null = 'Enter the time the consignment will leave Great Britain'
fields.notes: Legacy field the trace did not surface as a separate row (folded into the date display). Mandatory for CHED-D in the non-internal-market branch.

fields.name: commodityCode
fields.label: Commodity code
fields.hint: Change link -> /commodity/details?fromImporterReview=true#commodity-change-link-0 (one row per commodity)
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:303-313 (commodities @NotNull groups include NotificationSingleCedValidation); ValidationMessages.properties:74 commodities.not.null = 'Commodities'
fields.notes: Legacy: at least one commodity is mandatory for GB CHED-D submission. required=true reflects the commodities @NotNull; display-only here.

fields.name: consignmentGoodsTable
fields.label: Review Consignment 0 (Net weight / Packages / Package type)
fields.hint: Row: 1 kg/units | 1 | Bag
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ComplementParameterSet.java:73-80 net_weight (SingleCed) -> ValidationMessages.properties:132 'Add the net weight'; :110-115 number_package (SingleCed) -> :126 'Add number of packages'; :126-131 type_package (SingleCed) -> :125 'Add package type'
fields.notes: Legacy: per-consignment net weight, number of packages and package type are each mandatory for GB CHED-D submission (SingleCed key-data-pair validators).

fields.name: goodsTotals
fields.label: Total net weight / Total packages / Total gross weight
fields.hint: Totals block below the goods table
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: Commodities.java:82-99 totalNetWeight (Chedpp+SingleCed) -> ValidationMessages.properties:85 'Add the total net weight'; :63-80 totalGrossWeight (Chedpp+SingleCed) -> :84 'Add the total gross weight'; gross<net guard :83 'Total gross weight is less than the total net weight'
fields.notes: Legacy: total net weight and total gross weight are mandatory for GB CHED-D; the gross-weight field additionally fails if it is less than the total net weight. 'Total packages' displayed here is derived from the per-consignment package counts.

fields.name: portOfEntry

fields.hint: Under 'Transport to the port of entry' (Change -> /transport/before-bip)

fields.name: meansOfTransportToPortOfEntry
fields.label: Means of transport to port of entry
fields.conditionalOn: Transport-to-BCP captured (partOne.transporterDetailsRequired = true). The CED submit only adds NotificationCedFieldValidation when transport details are required.
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:503-529 (meansOfTransportFromEntryPoint @NotNull groups include NotificationCedFieldValidation); PartOneFieldValidator.java:162-165 adds NotificationCedFieldValidation only when transporterDetailsRequired; ValidationMessages.properties:60 meansOfTransportFromEntryPoint.not.null = 'Means of transport to the BCP'
fields.notes: Legacy: mandatory for GB CHED-D only when transport-to-BCP details are being provided (transporterDetailsRequired). The legacy error label reads 'Means of transport to the BCP' though the review section header is 'Transport to the port of entry'.

fields.name: transportIdentification
fields.label: Transport identification
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ValidationMessages.properties:43 meansoftransportfromentrypoint.id.singleced.not.empty = 'Add transport identification details'; :48 ...type.singleced.not.null = 'Add the means of transport to the port of entry' (singleced-scoped transport-to-entry-point copy exists)
fields.notes: Legacy: singleced-scoped 'not empty' copy exists for the transport identification field, part of the transport-to-BCP sub-form. The exact field-level @NotEmpty group binding lives on MeansOfTransportBeforeBip (not read in full here); requiredness is conditional on the transport-to-BCP section being provided. required left false pending confirmation of the sub-field group.

fields.name: usingRoadTrailersOrContainers
fields.label: Using road trailers or containers?
fields.notes: No CED-group @NotNull found for this flag in PartOne; treated as optional in legacy.

fields.name: transportDocumentReference
fields.label: Transport document reference
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ValidationMessages.properties:55 meansoftransportfromentrypoint.document.not.empty = 'Document' (document reference copy exists on the transport-to-entry-point form)
fields.notes: Legacy: transport document reference copy exists for the transport-to-BCP sub-form; binding to the CED submit group is conditional on that section. required left false pending sub-field group confirmation.

fields.name: estimatedArrivalDate
fields.label: Estimated arrival date at BCP or Port of entry
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:417-423 (arrivalDate @NotNull groups = HighRiskEuCed + NotificationSingleCedValidation); ValidationMessages.properties:69 arrivaldate.euced.not.null = 'Add the estimated arrival date at port of entry'
fields.notes: Legacy: mandatory for GB CHED-D submission. A cross-field guard also rejects an arrival date/time in the past (ValidationMessages.properties:71 'Enter an estimated arrival date and time at BCP that is not in the past').

fields.name: usingCTC

fields.name: usingGVMS
fields.label: Using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:650-657 (isGVMSRoute @NotNull group = NotificationGvmsRouteValidation); PartOneFieldValidator.java:159 adds NotificationGvmsRouteValidation for every CED submit; ValidationMessages.properties:163 isGVMSRoute.not.null = 'Select if using the Goods Vehicle Movement Service (GVMS)'
fields.notes: Legacy: mandatory for GB CHED-D submission (always in the CED group set).

fields.name: contactDetailsName
fields.label: Name
fields.hint: Under 'Contact details' (Change -> /responsible-person/contact-details)
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:625-639 (contactDetails object @NotNull groups include NotificationSingleCedValidation) -> ValidationMessages.properties:198 contactdetails.not.null = 'Enter your contact details'; ContactDetails.java:24-37 name @NotNull, :194 contactdetails.name.not.null = 'Enter your name'
fields.notes: Legacy: the contactDetails object is mandatory for GB CHED-D submission (SingleCed). The name-specific and email-or-telephone-specific sub-validators are bound to the EU/CHEDPP groups (ContactDetails.java:21-22, :24-37) rather than SingleCed, so on the GB CHED-D path the sub-field enforcement mechanism differs — flagged as a mandatoriness nuance for a human. required=true reflects the object-level requirement.

fields.name: contactDetailsEmail
fields.label: Email address
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ContactDetails.java:21 @ContactDetailsEmailOrTelephoneRequired(groups = NotificationContactDetailsEuFieldValidation); ValidationMessages.properties:196 contactdetails.email.or.telephone.not.null = 'Enter an email address or mobile number'
fields.notes: Legacy: email OR telephone must be provided ('Enter an email address or mobile number'). The enforcing validator is bound to the EU group, not SingleCed, so requiredness on the GB CHED-D path is a nuance for a human; required left false at the individual-field level (it is one half of an email-or-telephone rule).

fields.name: contactDetailsPhone
fields.label: Phone number
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ContactDetails.java:21 @ContactDetailsEmailOrTelephoneRequired; ValidationMessages.properties:196 contactdetails.email.or.telephone.not.null = 'Enter an email address or mobile number'
fields.notes: Legacy: one half of the email-or-telephone rule (see contactDetailsEmail). Individually optional; the pair is jointly required.

fields.name: nominatedContacts
fields.label: Summary of nominated contacts (Name / Email address / Mobile number)
fields.hint: Change -> /nominated-contact?fromImporterReview=true; no data rows in the observed traces
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:641-644 (nominatedContacts @NotEmpty group = NotificationTransporterContactDetailsEuFieldValidation only); ValidationMessages.properties:201 transportercontactdetails.not.empty = 'You must add at least one transport's contact details'
fields.notes: Legacy: nominated/transporter contacts are @NotEmpty only under an EU transporter-contact group, which is NOT part of the GB CHED-D (CED) submit set — hence the empty table submitted cleanly. required left false for GB CHED-D.

fields.name: contactAddressForConsignment
fields.label: Contact address for consignment
fields.hint: Change -> /organisation-branch-address?fromImporterReview=true (element ids #organisation-change-link / contact-address block)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 100; change-link id ReviewNotificationPage.ts:125-127
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ValidationMessages.properties:5-6 personResponsible.address.eucveda.not.empty / .eucvedp.not.empty = 'Add the contact address for consignment' (the empty-state link copy); :4 personResponsible.address.not.empty = 'Organisation Address'
fields.notes: Legacy: the 'Add the contact address for consignment' copy is bound to the EU CVEDA/CVEDP groups; no SingleCed binding for the person-responsible address was found, so GB CHED-D requiredness of this address is unclear — flagged as a nuance for a human. required left false.

fields.name: addContactAddressLink
fields.label: Add the contact address for consignment
fields.hint: EMPTY-STATE variant of the 'Contact address for consignment' section: when no branch/contact address is set, the review renders an 'Add the contact address for consignment' link instead of an address value. Not exercised in our traces (both had an address). Legacy CONFIRMS this exact copy string.
fields.conditionalOn: No contact address for consignment set on the notification
fields.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:5-6 personResponsible.address.eucveda.not.empty / .eucvedp.not.empty = 'Add the contact address for consignment' (verbatim match with ReviewNotificationPage.ts:157-159 linkAddContactAddress name)
fields.notes: Upgraded from inferred to legacy: the link label 'Add the contact address for consignment' matches the IPAFFS validation copy verbatim.

fields.name: importRiskCategory
fields.label: Import risk category
fields.hint: A summary-list row exposed by the shared review page object (dt 'Import risk category' + dd.review-summary-list__value). NOT observed in either CHED-D trace, and the CHED-D creation workflow never visits a risk-category page — may be a CHED-P/other-type row on this shared page rather than a CHED-D requirement.
fields.notes: No CED-group validation binding for a risk category was found in the model; consistent with this being a non-CHED-D row on the shared review page object.

fields.name: transportContacts
fields.label: Transport contacts
fields.hint: A 'Transport contacts' section heading with its own Change link (#transporter-contacts-change-link) exposed by the page object but NOT rendered in either trace (no transport contact was added in the journey). Variant/optional section.
fields.conditionalOn: Transport contact(s) present on the notification
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: PartOne.java:641-644; ValidationMessages.properties:201-202 — transporter contacts and storeTransporterContact are only enforced under the EU transporter-contact group, not the CED submit set
fields.notes: Legacy: transport contacts are not mandatory for GB CHED-D submission (the enforcing group is EU-only).

fields.name: permanentAddresses
fields.label: Permanent addresses
fields.hint: A 'Permanent addresses' sub-section (govuk-heading-m) exposed by the page object but NOT rendered in either trace. Variant-only section.
fields.conditionalOn: Permanent addresses present on the notification
fields.mandatorinessEvidence: ValidationMessages.properties:136 commodities.complementParameterSet.identifiers.permanentAddress.not.null = 'Add permanent address' — bound to CVEDA (animal) identifier flows, not the CED submit set
fields.notes: Legacy: 'Add permanent address' copy is a CVEDA animal-identifier concept, not a GB CHED-D requirement. Consistent with this being a non-CHED-D section on the shared review.

variantControls.name: btnSaveAndContinue
variantControls.label: Save and continue
variantControls.behaviour: Primary submit; proceeds to the Declaration page. Rendered only when displayChangeLinks is true (draft/edit context) and not gated by overdue-debt.

variantControls.name: btnAmend
variantControls.label: Amend

Legacy: a secondary button (govuk-button--secondary) posting to /review, shown when displayAmendButton and NOT displayChangeLinks. id=amend-certificate-{referenceNumber}.
variantControls.notes: Upgraded to legacy: the amend button copy is literally 'Amend' and it is a secondary button in the read-only (non-change-links) view.

variantControls.name: linkReviewAndSubmit
variantControls.label: Review and submit

variantControls.name: linkSplitConsignment
variantControls.label: Split consignment
variantControls.behaviour: Link (styled as govuk-button) to split a consignment's commodities across multiple CHEDs. Legacy: rendered only when displaySplitButton, href=/protected/notifications/{ref}/split-consignment, id=split-consignment-button. A preceding warning ('...you must split this consignment') shows when displaySplitWarning.
variantControls.notes: Upgraded to legacy: split-consignment is a real button on the shared review template gated by displaySplitButton; whether it is ever enabled for CHED-D specifically remains a gap.

variantControls.name: commoditySplitMessage
variantControls.label: Valid commodities split into - {ref} / Rejected commodities split into - {ref}
variantControls.behaviour: Status caption(s) shown when the consignment's commodities have been split (notification.splitConsignment present). Also renders an inset 'This consignment has now been split.' warning.
variantControls.evidence: review.html:195-211 (notification.splitConsignment block, govuk-caption-m 'Valid commodities split into - ' / 'Rejected commodities split into - '); ReviewNotificationPage.ts:26-28 (txtCommodityMessage /commodities split into -/i)
variantControls.notes: Upgraded to legacy: exact split-status caption copy is 'Valid commodities split into - {validReferenceNumber}' and 'Rejected commodities split into - {rejectedReferenceNumber}'.

behaviours.rule: The review page renders a govuk-error-summary at the top (unless overdue-debtor) whenever the notification has outstanding validation errors, listing one link per offending field (href=#{field}) so the user can jump to fix it. Default title 'Please fix the following errors'. Renders NOTHING when there are no errors (both happy-path traces).

For a CHED-D (CED) notification, the fields that block Save and continue are assembled per notification type: always NotificationSingleCedValidation + ProvideCtcMrnValidation + NotificationGvmsRouteValidation; NotificationCedFieldValidation + TransporterDetailsRequiredCEDValidation are added when transporterDetailsRequired; and the non-internal-market landbridge groups (NotNullPointOfExit / NotNullEstimatedArrivalDate / NotNullEstimatedArrivalTime) are added when the non-internal-market branch applies.
The review page renders at least 11 'Change' links (one per amendable summary section).

The review page reflects the CURRENT (post-amend) notification data: after amending country of origin to 'India' the review shows 'India', and it still shows 'India' when re-opened read-only after submission.

Change links deep-link back into the edit journey with stable element ids: #country-of-origin-change-link, #traders-change-link, #organisation-change-link, #responsible-person-contact-details-change-link, #transport-to-bip-change-link, #goods-movement-services-change-link, #transporter-contacts-change-link, #commodity-change-bulk-link-0.

When the notification originates from an external company system (isNotificationFromApi) a warning 'Information updated here will not be sent back to your company system' is shown; when it is a clone/external-system that needs correcting, the error-summary title is overridden to 'You need to update some details' (cloned CVEDA) or 'Some information needs to be updated' (other external systems).

validationMessages.message: Please fix the following errors
validationMessages.context: Default govuk-error-summary title on the review page when the notification has outstanding validation errors blocking Save and continue.

validationMessages.message: You need to update some details
validationMessages.helpText: Some details are missing or incorrect from the health certificate you cloned. You need to update these before you can submit this notification.
validationMessages.context: Error-summary title + help text when the notification is a cloned CVEDA (isExternalSystem + isCveda).

validationMessages.message: Some information needs to be updated
validationMessages.context: Error-summary title for other cloned / external-system notifications that need correcting.

validationMessages.message: Port of entry and port of exit are both {designatedBip}
validationMessages.context: Special error rendered at the top of the review error-summary when the chosen port of entry equals the port of exit; offers change-port-of-entry / change-port-of-exit links.

validationMessages.message: Enter an email address or mobile number
validationMessages.context: Either email OR telephone required (validator bound to the EU contact-details group).

validationMessages.message: You must add at least one transport's contact details
validationMessages.context: Only enforced under the EU transporter-contact group — NOT part of the GB CHED-D (CED) submit set, so an empty table submits cleanly for CHED-D.

openQuestions: This is a read-only summary/review page: it has NO data-entry controls. The only interactive elements are the primary 'Save and continue' button, three 'Copy' buttons, and per-section 'Change' links. The 'fields' array therefore enumerates the read-only summary items the review must DISPLAY (control:'text' = rendered display value), not inputs — this tells the rebuild what data the review page must render. observedValues are the concrete values seen in the two traces. The 'required' flag on each field now encodes whether the UNDERLYING notification datum is mandatory for GB CHED-D (CED) submission (legacy @NotNull), NOT whether the review renders an input — see each field's mandatorinessEvidence.

LEGACY RESOLVED (was a gap): the review page CAN surface validation/error state. review.html:50-64 renders {{> common/validationSummaryReview}} — a govuk-error-summary listing each outstanding validation error as a link to the offending section. It is simply empty on a clean notification, which is why neither happy-path trace showed it. Default title 'Please fix the following errors'; cloned/external-system variants override the title/help text (review.html:52-59).

Conditional rows confirmed: 'Point of exit' and 'When the consignment will leave Great Britain' appear under 'About the consignment' ONLY when 'Main reason for importing the consignment' = 'Non-internal market' (trace b3742...). They are absent for 'For internal market' (trace eddc6...). LEGACY confirms these are additionally MANDATORY in that branch (PartOneFieldValidator.java:167-171 adds the landbridge NotNull groups), and that the datetime is stored as two mandatory fields — date + time (Purpose.java:81-94) — the review folds both into one displayed value.

The 'Nominated contacts' table rendered its header row but no data rows in both traces (no nominated contacts were added). LEGACY confirms nominated/transporter contacts are NOT mandatory for GB CHED-D (the @NotEmpty group is EU-only, ValidationMessages.properties:201), so the empty table submits cleanly. The data-row shape (Name / Email address / Mobile number) is inferred from the column headers only.

Page uses a bespoke 'review-summary-list' component for most sections rather than the standard govuk-summary-list (only the reference/code header block uses the real component). The rebuild should consolidate all of these onto the standard GOV.UK Summary list.
The service sub-header shows a 'Back' link with href '#' (JS-driven history back) plus a 'Dashboard' breadcrumb — this is IPAFFS chrome around the page, not an in-main govuk-back-link.
The caption/H1 sit inside a full-width column; a review/summary page in the new app would typically use two-thirds width, but IPAFFS renders it full-width to fit the wide tables.

ReviewNotificationPage.ts is a SHARED page object across all four CHED types, so some getters it exposes may not apply to CHED-D: 'Import risk category' (riskCategoryValue, :69-71) — no CED-group validation binds a risk category in the model, consistent with it being a non-CHED-D row; 'Catch certificate reference' table (catchCertificateSummaryTable, :73-79) is a CHED-P (fishery/POAO) concept and was deliberately NOT added as a CHED-D field. LEGACY also shows 'Permanent addresses' copy ('Add permanent address', ValidationMessages.properties:136) is a CVEDA animal-identifier concept, not CHED-D. A human should still confirm whether Import risk category / Transport contacts / Permanent addresses ever appear on the CHED-D review specifically.

MANDATORINESS NUANCE for a human: countryOfOrigin and countryFromWhereConsigned (consignedCountry) are NOT tagged @NotNull under the GB single CHED-D (SingleCed) group in the model (Commodities.java:168-201) — the old system did not block CHED-D submission on either at that level, even though they are mandatory for the EU/other-risk CHED-D groups. If the rebuild wants them mandatory for CHED-D this is a deliberate policy change, not a like-for-like port.

MANDATORINESS NUANCE for a human: contact details name and email-or-telephone sub-field validators are bound to the EU/CHEDPP contact-details groups (ContactDetails.java:21-22), NOT to SingleCed. For GB CHED-D only the contactDetails OBJECT is @NotNull (PartOne.java:625-639). So the exact sub-field enforcement on the GB CHED-D path differs from the EU path — worth confirming the intended behaviour.

Amend variant: opening a submitted CHED-D for amendment surfaces a secondary 'Amend' button (review.html:101-111, id=amend-certificate-{ref}) in the read-only (non-change-links) view, plus a 'Review and submit' link on the hub. LEGACY confirms the 'Amend' button copy and that it is a secondary button. The create-flow trace only shows the 'Save and continue' primary button (rendered when displayChangeLinks is true, review.html:344-352).

Split-consignment variant: LEGACY confirms a real 'Split consignment' button (review.html:189-193, id=split-consignment-button, gated by displaySplitButton) and split-status captions ('Valid commodities split into - {ref}', 'Rejected commodities split into - {ref}', review.html:195-211). Whether CHED-D ever enables splitting is still a gap for a human.

CUC (Common User Charge) variant: the ched-d-workflows isCuc path adds billing pages (confirm-billing-details) before review (ched-d-workflows.ts:355-362), but no billing/charge summary section is exposed on the review page object and none rendered in our (non-CUC) traces. The model carries billingInformation / commonUserCharge on PartOne (PartOne.java:658-661) but without CED-group @NotNull. Whether the review page shows a billing summary for CUC notifications is a gap.

Test-stated requirement (inferred, ched-d-manipulation.spec.ts:51-52): the review page must render at least 11 'Change' links. Our single detailed trace enumerated Change links per section; the '>=11' floor is a human-authored assertion, not a rendered count we independently verified against the trace — recorded as a behaviour, confidence inferred.
